title: Screen recording (⌘⇧5): region/full, audio device, floating stop, into history
goal: TOP-PRIORITY per the user: screen-video capture is one of the most important features. Match CleanShot's ⌘⇧5. Use ScreenCaptureKit (SCStream) — the modern macOS path for screen + audio, not the deprecated AVCaptureScreenInput. Must let the user pick the SCREEN REGION (full/region/window), choose AUDIO INPUTS (system and/or mic), and record to MP4 plus GIF and animated WebP. Recordings land in the same history bin as screenshots (phase 11). Recording session/config logic lives in a testable core type; AVAssetWriter/SCStream plumbing is app-side.

tasks.title: ⌘⇧5 recording entry: choose whole screen or a region
tasks.notes: DONE 2026-06-21. Global ⌘⇧5 (HotkeyCenter) + menu items (menu-bar dropdown 'Record Screen / Video…' and Capture menu, both flipping to 'Stop Recording' while live) → CaptureCenter.toggleRecording → beginRecordingFlow presents RecordingSetupController (a key-capable centered NSPanel hosting RecordingSetupView: Full Screen vs Region… segmented + audio + Start). Region reuses the existing RectSelectionController (drag, then record exactly that rect on its screen); full records the active screen. DEVIATION from the literal plan: used SCStream + the modern SCRecordingOutput (macOS 15+) instead of hand-rolled AVAssetWriter — it writes the MP4 itself (video+audio) to a temp file; far less plumbing. ScreenRecorder.start(config:screen:to:excluding:) builds SCStreamConfiguration (sourceRect+scale for region, showsCursor, 60fps cap) + SCContentFilter, adds the recording output, startCapture. VERIFIED: builds against the real SDK; the post-record pipeline (poster/history/GIF/HEIC) validated headlessly via a synthetic MP4. NEEDS USER: the actual ⌘⇧5 record on a granted machine (Screen Recording TCC denied on the build box).

tasks.title: Audio device picker (system audio and/or microphone)
tasks.notes: DONE 2026-06-21. RecordingSetupView offers a 'System Audio' toggle + a 'Microphone' picker (None + each device, from ScreenRecorder.availableMicrophones() = AVCaptureDevice.DiscoverySession over [.microphone,.external]). Both independent (AudioSources OptionSet, PhotonzCore, TDD). No AVAssetWriter muxing needed — SCStreamConfiguration.capturesAudio (system) + captureMicrophone/microphoneCaptureDeviceID (mic, macOS 15+) feed SCRecordingOutput directly. Last choice persisted as RecordingConfig JSON in UserDefaults (RecordingCoordinator.config); a saved mic is only re-offered if still attached. Bundle Info.plist gains NSMicrophoneUsageDescription (build-app.sh) so mic access doesn't crash. NEEDS USER: confirm system+mic audio land in the file and the mic-permission prompt appears.

tasks.title: Floating stop control that is excluded from the recording
tasks.notes: DONE 2026-06-21. RecordingControlsController shows a borderless, non-activating (.statusBar level) Liquid-Glass HUD top-center of the recording screen: pulsing red dot + monospaced elapsed time (RecordingClock.elapsedString, PhotonzCore TDD; ticked every 0.25s by RecordingCoordinator's Timer) + a red Stop button. The HUD is shown BEFORE startCapture (so the window server knows it) and its NSWindow is passed to ScreenRecorder.start(excluding:) → SCContentFilter(display:excludingWindows:) maps NSWindow.windowNumber → SCWindow.windowID and excludes it, so it never appears in the video. Stop → recorder.stop() awaits SCRecordingOutput finalization (delegate didFinishRecording, with a 400ms fallback) then files the asset. NEEDS USER: confirm the HUD shows during a real recording and is absent from the resulting file.

tasks.title: Recordings drop into the history bin alongside images
tasks.notes: DONE 2026-06-21. CaptureEntry gained kind (CaptureKind .image/.video, defaults .image with custom Decodable so pre-12 index.json decodes unchanged) + duration + posterFileName + fileExtension (png/mp4) — TDD (5 new entry tests incl. legacy decode + mixed history). CaptureStore.addRecording(tempURL:duration:poster:) moves the finalized MP4 into the library + writes a poster PNG; image(for:) returns the poster for videos; copyToPasteboard puts the FILE URL (not a frame) for videos; remove cleans up both files. RecordingCoordinator generates duration+poster (VideoExporter, AVAssetImageGenerator) on stop, files the entry, and fires onRecordingComplete → the SAME Quick Access Overlay path screenshots use. Overlays (HistoryOverlay + QuickAccessOverlay) now render video cells via shared CaptureThumbnailView (play badge + duration pill) with video-specific actions: Copy / Play (NSWorkspace.open the MP4 — in-app video editing is phase 13) / Save+Export menu / Delete. VERIFIED headlessly: synthetic-MP4 self-test confirmed addRecording → mp4+poster on disk, thumbnail loads, entry kind=video. NEEDS USER: a real recording appearing in the bin + Quick Access with the play affordance.

tasks.title: Record to MP4 + GIF + animated HEIC (WebP deferred)
tasks.notes: DONE 2026-06-21. Quick-convert path (per the plan's 'or as a quick convert'): the recording is always MP4 in history; the video cells' Save/Export menu offers Save Video (MP4)… / Export GIF… / Export HEIC…. VideoExporter.exportAnimated re-encodes recorded frames via AVAssetImageGenerator + ImageIO CGImageDestination — GIF (kCGImagePropertyGIFDictionary, loop 0, per-frame unclamped delay) and animated HEIC (public.heics container + kCGImagePropertyHEICSDictionary). Frame count / delay / downscale come from AnimatedExportPlanner (PhotonzCore, TDD; reuses PinnedImageMetrics.fittedSize, default 15fps/800px cap). DEVIATION (user-approved 2026-06-21): WebP DROPPED — macOS 26's built-in ImageIO can't WRITE WebP (CGImageDestinationCopyTypeIdentifiers lists only gif + heic/heics for animation; org.webmproject.webp is read-only). Adding it needs a vendored libwebp C dep, which conflicts with the SwiftPM-only/no-deps stance — user chose GIF + animated HEIC instead; WebP → backlog. VERIFIED headlessly: a 1s synthetic MP4 exported to GIF (15 frames, 5.7KB) and HEIC (15 frames, 10.7KB), both valid multi-frame animations. RecordingFormat keeps .format for the future record-time choice; trim/crop sharing with phase 13 is still pending (this is record→export, not record→trim→export yet).
